texto: BearingsYou are my dear compass,who knows no way but true,so when I'm lost and drifting,I find myself in you.Yet when I ask you, fearful,if I should set you free,imagine my surprise to hearyou take your north from me.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Louise Hawes sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “BearingsYou are my dear compass,who knows no way but true,so when I'm lost and drifting,I find my…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, bearings, compass, útil em momentos de compass e direction.

texto: For MargaretSome people laughha-ha-ha.Other people puttheir hands on their mouthshe-he-he.In the department stores Santa laughsho-ho-ho.But this girl I know−okay, this girl I'm crazy forlaughs like an envelope tearing open and good stuffspilling out.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Louise Hawes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. texto: Safe DepositI thought that I could keep it−the light on the running tide,how your eyes give you awayno matter what you hide.I thought that I could hold it−the forest along the sand,your neck bones like pearlsunderneath my hand.But time's school has taught mehow petals brown and die.There's no saving pleasure.Don't try. Don't try.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Louise Hawes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. texto: It was like diving into winter waves. "I can't," I told him."Why not?""Because I need to find out who I am by myself before I can be with anyone else.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Louise Hawes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It was like diving into winter waves.
